title: dodonaea viscosa subsp. burmanniana
intro: Dodonaea viscosa subsp. burmanniana, also known as the Sticky Hopbush or Burmann's Sticky Hopbush, is a subspecies of the Dodonaea viscosa plant, native to tropical regions of Australia and the Pacific Islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dodonaea viscosa subsp. burmanniana include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
